name: Principled
description: Principled characters are, generally, the strong, moral character. Superman is of principled alignment, with the highest regard for others' lives and well-being, truth and honor. They will always attempt to work with and within the law. Principled characters will: always keep their word; avoid lies; never kill or attack an unarmed foe; never harm an innocent; never torture for any reason; never kill for pleasure; always help others; always work within the law whenever possible; never break the law unless conditions are desperate; respect authority, law, self-discipline and honor; work well in a group; never take dirty money or items; and never betray a friend.

name: Scrupulous
description: Scrupulous characters value life and freedom above all else, and despise those who would deprive others of them. Typically portrayed as the person forced to work beyond the law yet for the greater good, they are not vicious or vindictive but are driven to right injustice. They will always attempt to work with or within the law whenever possible. Scrupulous characters will: keep their word to any other good person; lie only to people of selfish or evil alignments; never attack or kill an unarmed foe; never harm an innocent; never torture for pleasure but may use muscle to extract information from criminals; never kill for pleasure and always attempt to bring the villain to justice alive; always help others; attempt to work within the law whenever possible; bend and occasionally break the law when deemed necessary; distrust authority but try not to blatantly break the law; work with groups but dislike confining bureaucracy; never take dirty money or items; and never betray a friend.

name: Unprincipled
description: This basically good person tends to be selfish, greedy, and holds personal freedom and welfare above almost everything else. They dislike confining laws and distrust authority, viewing the law as well-intentioned but clumsy and ineffective. Often a vigilante type whose intentions are good but who finds the law too confining, working outside it for what they see as greater efficiency. May take dirty money from known criminals and associate with both good and evil characters. Unprincipled characters will: keep their word of honor; lie and cheat if necessary especially to anarchist and evil alignments; not kill an unarmed foe but will take advantage of one; never harm an innocent; not use torture unless absolutely necessary; never kill for pleasure; usually help those in need; rarely attempt to work within the law; blatantly break the law to achieve crimefighting goals; distrust authority and bureaucracy; work with groups especially if it serves their needs; take dirty money; never betray a friend; and have a high regard for life and freedom.

name: Anarchist
description: The insurgent, con-man, gambler and high roller. An uncommitted freebooter more likely to be a crimefighter for the thrill of danger than any cause. Will at least consider doing anything if the price is right or the challenge great. Attracted to the impossible, dangerous, and the underdog. Intrigued by power, glory and wealth. Laws and rules infringe on personal freedom and were meant to be broken. Usually the daring vigilante or anti-hero who feels the end justifies the means, always looking for the best deal and self-gratification. Anarchist characters will: may keep their word; lie and cheat if they feel it necessary; not likely to kill an unarmed foe but will attack or beat one; never kill an innocent but may harm or kidnap; use torture to extract information but not likely for pleasure; seldom kill for pleasure; rarely help without an ulterior motive; rarely work within the law; constantly break the law to achieve goals; have little respect for authority or self-discipline; not work well within groups; take dirty money without hesitation; and may betray a friend.

name: Miscreant
description: This self-serving, unscrupulous character is out only for himself. Power, glory, wealth, position, and anything that will make his life more comfortable or pleasurable is the goal. It doesn't matter who gets caught in the middle as long as he comes out on top. Will lie, cheat and hurt anyone to attain personal goals. If a miscreant becomes a vigilante or hero it will be for some personal reason or vendetta. Miscreant characters will: not necessarily keep their word to anyone; lie and cheat indiscriminately; kill an unarmed foe as readily as a competitor; use or harm an innocent; use torture for extracting information and pleasure; may kill for sheer pleasure; feel no compulsion to help without tangible reward; work within the law only if necessary; blatantly break the law for personal goals; dislike and distrust authority; work with others only to attain personal goals; take dirty money and hot goods without hesitation; betray a friend if it serves their needs; and have little respect for others' lives.

name: Aberrant
description: Driven to attain goals through force, power, and intimidation, yet standing apart from other evil characters because of a personal code of ethics. Expects loyalty from minions, punishing disloyalty with a swift, merciful death. Will always keep their word of honor and uphold bargains. If an anti-hero, will completely disregard the law and deal out justice as they see fit — never cruel or vindictive, but always absolutely certain of guilt before acting. Once a person is condemned they will be destroyed without mercy. Looks upon people without honor or loyalty as worthless. Aberrant characters will: always keep their word of honor; lie to and cheat those not worthy of respect; may or may not kill an unarmed foe; never kill an innocent particularly a child but may harm, harass or kidnap; never torture for pleasure but will use it to extract information; never kill for pleasure; may or may not help someone in need; rarely attempt to work within the law; break the law without hesitation; respect honor and self-discipline but have no use for bureaucracy; work with others to attain goals; may take dirty money; and never betray a friend.

name: Diabolic
description: The category where megalomaniacs, violent, and most despicable characters fall. A cruel, brutal killer who trusts no one and has no value for any life other than their own. Will crush anyone or anything that gets in the way, lying, cheating, using and abusing anyone not more powerful than themselves. Even aberrant characters find these dishonorable people revolting. Diabolic characters will: rarely keep their word and have no honor; lie to and cheat anyone; most certainly attack and kill an unarmed foe; hurt and kill an innocent without a second thought or for pleasure; use torture for pleasure and information; kill for sheer pleasure; help someone only on a whim; rarely attempt to work within the law; blatantly break the law; despise honor, authority and self-discipline viewing them as weaknesses; not work well within a group constantly vying for power; always take dirty money, hot goods and drugs; betray a friend; and associate mostly with other evil alignments.
